summary: Iteration 2: Comprehensive testing of new features (quote modal, floating widget, service pages, data recovery section, animated counters, updated contact details) all PASSED. Backend pytest 5/5. Frontend Playwright e2e covered every scenario in the review request — 100% pass.
critical_code_review_comments: FloatingWidgets.jsx QuoteModal: client-side validation only checks name/phone/device_model/issue but backend POST /api/repair-requests requires `email` too (Pydantic). If a user leaves email empty in the quote modal, frontend will send email='' which currently passes (since EmailStr is NOT used). Consider validating/normalising email or making email truly optional server-side (Optional[str]) to align with the UX which advertises 'Email (optional)'.
Backend POST /api/repair-requests still has no email format validation (Pydantic EmailStr) — verified again in this iteration.
POST /api/repair-requests is unauthenticated and unrate-limited; the public quote modal exposes new abuse surface. Consider basic rate-limiting / captcha for production.

Counter animation in Trust.jsx runs every time the Reveal becomes visible; values briefly show interim numbers (e.g. 19+, 66,962+) before settling. Final values verified correct (22+, 75,000+, 38,000+). Not a bug, just expected easing.

ServicePage.jsx 'Get a free quote' (service-cta-primary) and 'Request a repair' (service-cta-band) link to /#repair instead of dispatching 'open-quote' — slightly inconsistent with the new floating quote modal pattern, but acceptable.
FAQ idx=0 is always open on mount; pressing faq-toggle-0 correctly toggles. Verified.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 additional TEST_QM_<timestamp> repair-request via the quote modal during E2E. GET /api/repair-requests confirmed persistence (total 7 records).
verified_features: Homepage loads with why-us section + animated counters (22+, 75,000+, 38,000+, 24/7) and data-recovery-section
fab-quote opens quote-modal; clicking submit with empty fields shows 'Please fill in...' error toast and modal stays open
Filling required fields + submit: success toast 'Quote request sent!' appears, modal closes, record persisted (verified via GET /api/repair-requests)
data-recovery-quote button dispatches 'open-quote' event and opens the same quote modal
data-recovery-learn navigates to /services/data-recovery and renders ServicePage with H1, breadcrumb, FAQ accordion (faq-toggle-0 toggles), updated document.title

All 7 service pages render correctly with correct H1, meta title, breadcrumb: mobile-phone-repair, iphone-repair, laptop-pc-repair, data-recovery, phone-unlocking, tablet-ipad-repair, samsung-galaxy-repair
Home 'all-service-*' links navigate to correct /services/:slug routes
Footer contains '60 Keirby Walk', 'BB11 2DE', '01282 761818'; nav also shows '01282 761818'
Existing repair-form (data-testid='repair-form') still present and reachable via /#repair
context_for_next_testing_agent: All review_request scenarios verified at https://brand-landing-v2.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend endpoint POST/GET /api/repair-requests stable. Two notable code-quality items remain (no email format validation, no rate-limit) — see critical_code_review_comments. Test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_repair_requests.py (5 tests).
